title: What is your fav EOE track
display_title: What is your fav EOE track
wikitext:
[]: What is your fav EOE track?             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Thu Jul 13, 2006 7:52 pm On the End of Evangelion soundtrack what track number is your fav? Mine is number 7. I think that the music and the scene it was played un fit so well. It was dark yet beautiful.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Jul 13, 2006 8:27 pm It's hard to pick, The End of Evangelion had such an amazing sound track. I think I'm going to have to go with track 14, "The crack in the dream". It's just such a nice short little piece. Beautiful and yet sad at the same time.

[]:             Quote Post by ChronoX » Thu Jul 13, 2006 8:30 pm Sadly, I don't have that CD. But my fav track from EOE is Tamashi No Refrain. Good Song

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Jul 13, 2006 8:32 pm ChronoX wrote: Sadly, I don't have that CD. But my fav track from EOE is Tamashi No Refrain. Good Song

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Thu Jul 13, 2006 8:33 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: ChronoX wrote: Sadly, I don't have that CD. But my fav track from EOE is Tamashi No Refrain. Good Song

[]:             Quote Post by ChronoX » Thu Jul 13, 2006 8:34 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: ChronoX wrote: Sadly, I don't have that CD. But my fav track from EOE is Tamashi No Refrain. Good Song

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Thu Jul 13, 2006 8:36 pm ChronoX wrote: The Eva Monkey wrote: ChronoX wrote: Sadly, I don't have that CD. But my fav track from EOE is Tamashi No Refrain. Good Song

[]:             Quote Post by ChronoX » Thu Jul 13, 2006 8:39 pm Evangelionfreak wrote: ChronoX wrote: The Eva Monkey wrote: ChronoX wrote: Sadly, I don't have that CD. But my fav track from EOE is Tamashi No Refrain. Good Song

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Thu Jul 13, 2006 8:41 pm ChronoX wrote: Evangelionfreak wrote: ChronoX wrote: The Eva Monkey wrote: ChronoX wrote: Sadly, I don't have that CD. But my fav track from EOE is Tamashi No Refrain. Good Song

[]:             Quote Post by ChronoX » Thu Jul 13, 2006 8:42 pm Evangelionfreak wrote: ChronoX wrote: Evangelionfreak wrote: ChronoX wrote: The Eva Monkey wrote: ChronoX wrote: Sadly, I don't have that CD. But my fav track from EOE is Tamashi No Refrain. Good Song

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Thu Jul 13, 2006 8:44 pm ChronoX wrote: Evangelionfreak wrote: ChronoX wrote: Evangelionfreak wrote: ChronoX wrote: The Eva Monkey wrote: ChronoX wrote: Sadly, I don't have that CD. But my fav track from EOE is Tamashi No Refrain. Good Song

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Fri Jul 14, 2006 2:29 am I'm quite torn between Expansion of the Blockade and Komm, Susser Todd.

[]:             Quote Post by ChronoX » Fri Jul 14, 2006 9:34 am Evangelionfreak wrote: ChronoX wrote: Evangelionfreak wrote: ChronoX wrote: Evangelionfreak wrote: ChronoX wrote: The Eva Monkey wrote: ChronoX wrote: Sadly, I don't have that CD. But my fav track from EOE is Tamashi No Refrain. Good Song

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Fri Jul 14, 2006 11:25 am I bought the OST, but haven't really listened through it yet. Remarkably good for a movie soundtrack, though. One mp3 that I've had for years is called "Heisoku no Kokudai". I think it was called "Expansion of the Blockade" in english. That one is real sweet. But "Komm Susser Todd" is my favourite, predictable enough. Alond with the classic pieces (much like Kaworu points out) it really gives off a sense of impending doom. At the brink of death, listening to these songs seem like a good way too end what little piece we have left before the painful apocalypse. That's why Kanon makes me so sad.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Fri Jul 14, 2006 12:55 pm Evangelionfreak wrote: www.discountanimedvd.com

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Fri Jul 14, 2006 2:49 pm drinian wrote: Evangelionfreak wrote: www.discountanimedvd.com

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Fri Jul 14, 2006 5:06 pm I apologize for taking this off-topic. Mods, feel free to delete or move. Rant begins now: Evangelionfreak wrote: well i just like the fact that they ar cheap. the subtitles are excellent. Ive never had a problem w/ them for 5 years. And I have lots of region 2 dvd's but since they have no subtitles. and they are more expensive I'd rather go with bootleg copies (i refuse to pay $89 for earthian when I can get it sor $9.99)

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Fri Jul 14, 2006 5:13 pm drinian wrote: I apologize for taking this off-topic. Mods, feel free to delete or move. Rant begins now: Evangelionfreak wrote: well i just like the fact that they ar cheap. the subtitles are excellent. Ive never had a problem w/ them for 5 years. And I have lots of region 2 dvd's but since they have no subtitles. and they are more expensive I'd rather go with bootleg copies (i refuse to pay $89 for earthian when I can get it sor $9.99)

[]: Re: What is your fav EOE track?             Quote Post by TheCarkolum » Wed Jun 28, 2017 7:02 pm Expansion of the Blockade. Elegant, cute, sad, hopeful, relaxing and amazing!

[]: Re: What is your fav EOE track?             Quote Post by pwhodges » Wed Jun 28, 2017 7:09 pm Eleven year necro - impressive! But how could the answer be anything other than Komm susser Tod?
